title: microlepia hookeriana var. marginalis
intro: Microlepia hookeriana var. marginalis, also known as the Marginal Microlepia or Marginal Lizard's Tail, is a variety of the Microlepia hookeriana fern species native to tropical regions of Asia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Microlepia hookeriana var. marginalis include reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing, as well as supporting immune function and reducing oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
